subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of key ideas and details in literature.
eligibleContentText: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text explicitly says and when drawing inferences from the text.
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 4 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of key ideas and details in literature.
eligibleContentText: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text; summarize the text.
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 4 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of key ideas and details in literature.
eligibleContentText: Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story, drama, or poem, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., a character's thoughts, words, or actions).
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 4 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of craft and structure in literature.
eligibleContentText: Compare and contrast the point of view from which different stories are narrated, including the difference between first- and third-person narrations.
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 4 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of connections within, between, and/or among texts.
eligibleContentText: Compare and contrast the treatment of similar themes and topics (e.g., opposition of good and evil) and patterns of events (e.g., the quest) in stories, myths, and traditional literature from different cultures.
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 4 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of vocabulary and figurative language in literature.
eligibleContentText: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 4 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies (context clues including allusions, Greek and Latin affixes and roots).
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 4 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of vocabulary and figurative language in literature.
eligibleContentText: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings (similes and metaphors; idioms, adages, and proverbs; antonyms and synonyms).
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 4 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of key ideas and details in informational texts.
eligibleContentText: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 4 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of key ideas and details in informational texts.
eligibleContentText: Determine the main idea of a text and explain how it is supported by key details; summarize the text.
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 4 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of key ideas and details in informational texts.
eligibleContentText: Explain events, procedures, ideas, steps, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text, including what happened and why, based on specific information in the text.
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 4 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of craft and structure in informational texts.
eligibleContentText: Compare and contrast a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event or topic; describe the differences in focus and the information provided.
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 4 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of craft and structure in informational texts.
eligibleContentText: Describe the overall structure (e.g., chronology, comparison, cause/effect, problem/solution) of events, ideas, concepts, or information and text features in a text or part of a text.
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 4 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of connections within, between, and/or among informational texts.
eligibleContentText: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text.
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 4 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of connections within, between, and/or among informational texts.
eligibleContentText: Integrate information from two texts on the same topic in order to demonstrate subject knowledge.
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 4 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of connections within, between, and/or among informational texts.
eligibleContentText: Interpret text features (e.g., headings, graphics, charts, timelines, diagrams) and/or make connections between text and the content of text features.
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 4 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of vocabulary and figurative language in informational texts.
eligibleContentText: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 4 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies (context clues, Greek and Latin affixes and roots, academic/domain words).
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 4 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate understanding of vocabulary and figurative language in informational texts.
eligibleContentText: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings (similes and metaphors; idioms, adages, and proverbs; antonyms and synonyms).
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 4 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage.
eligibleContentText: Use relative pronouns (e.g., who, whose, whom, which, that) and relative adverbs (e.g., where, when, why).
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 4 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage.
eligibleContentText: Form and use the progressive verb tenses (e.g., I was walking, I am walking, I will be walking).

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage.

anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage.
eligibleContentText: Order adjectives within sentences according to conventional patterns (e.g., a small red bag rather than a red small bag).

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage.
eligibleContentText: Form and use prepositional phrases.

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage.
eligibleContentText: Produce complete sentences, recognizing and correcting inappropriate fragments and run-on sentences.

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage.
eligibleContentText: Correctly use frequently confused words (e.g., to, too, two; there, their, they're).
sourceDocument: PDE Grade 4 ELA Assessment Anchors and Eligible Content

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage.
eligibleContentText: Ensure subject-verb and pronoun-antecedent agreement.

anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling.

anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling.

anchorDescriptorText: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling.
eligibleContentText: Spell grade-appropriate words correctly.

anchorDescriptorText: Use knowledge of language and its conventions.
eligibleContentText: Choose words and phrases to convey ideas precisely.

anchorDescriptorText: Use knowledge of language and its conventions.
eligibleContentText: Choose punctuation for effect.

anchorDescriptorText: Use knowledge of language and its conventions.

anchorDescriptorText: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and/or research.

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and/or research.
eligibleContentText: Develop the analysis using a variety of evidence from text(s) to support claims, opinions, ideas, and inferences.

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and/or research.
eligibleContentText: Link ideas within categories of information using words and phrases (e.g., another, for example, also, because).

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and/or research.
eligibleContentText: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic and/or convey the experience and events.

subject: ELA
anchorDescriptorText: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and/or research.
eligibleContentText: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the analysis presented.
